Definition: No-memory response equals zero residual against a selected baseline
Risk: Can hide baseline selection effects; only valid after baseline bracket policy is satisfied
RequiredNextCheck: Use as null comparator only until K2 response amplitude is registered.

Definition: No-memory response allows one global residual offset
Risk: Same-data constant offset absorbs baseline scale mismatch
RequiredNextCheck: Use as diagnostic control only; not a K2 baseline.

Definition: No-memory response from DESI BAO same-data posterior maximum
Risk: Too permissive because the baseline is optimized on the tested BAO vector
RequiredNextCheck: Use as same-data sensitivity control only.

Definition: No-memory response from public CMB-only best-fit baseline evaluated on DESI BAO rows
Risk: Independent baseline carries pre-existing BAO tension; K2 interpretation needs null benchmark
RequiredNextCheck: Run null and K2 protocol on the same covariance before any interpretation.

Definition: Future locked finite-memory response baseline exported before BAO scoring
Risk: Unavailable; without it K2 amplitude can become post-hoc
RequiredNextCheck: Define amplitude normalization and source before K2 scoring.
